title: 10 best herbal decoctions for amenorrhea
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for amenorrhea are a traditional approach to treating irregular or absent menstrual cycles, using a concentrated liquid extract made by steeping herbs in hot water. These decoctions help regulate menstruation by balancing hormones, stimulating uterine contractions, and addressing underlying conditions such as stress, fatigue, and nutrient deficiencies. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with amenorrhea include those made from raspberry leaf, ginger, and vitex, which have been shown to restore normal menstrual cycles and alleviate associated symptoms like cramping and mood swings. By restoring regular periods, these decoctions can improve overall quality of life for women suffering from amenorrhea, reducing anxiety and increasing confidence in one's reproductive health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree decoctions helps with amenorrhea because they contain phytoestrogens, specifically flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help regulate hormonal imbalances that can cause menstrual irregularities. The decoction's estrogenic properties stimulate the pituitary gland to release follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), promoting ovulation and menstruation. Additionally, chaste tree's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties may help alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with menstrual cycles, further supporting its use in treating amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-normalizing effects: Chasteberry (Vitex agnus-castus) helps regulate estrogen levels, which can alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.
Progestogenic activity: The herb stimulates the production of progesterone, a hormone essential for menstruation, which can help stimulate regular menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrheic symptoms.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Chasteberry's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to irregular menstrual cycles and amenorrhea.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, particularly aucubin and agnuside, in Vitex agnus-castus help with amenorrhea by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, thereby regulating menstrual cycles and hormone balance.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Vitex agnus-castus, such as kaempferol and quercetin, exhibit estrogenic and anti-estrogenic properties, which help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea by influencing the body's hormonal balance.

Diterpenes: Diterpenes, particularly agnuside and aucubin, in Vitex agnus-castus may help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing stress, and promoting hormonal balance.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the primary part used for decoctions to treat amenorrhea because they contain the highest concentration of iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have a regulatory effect on the menstrual cycle.
Leaves: The leaves are another commonly used part for decoctions due to their ability to enhance the overall effect of the flowers and provide a more sustained release of the active compounds.
Seeds: The seeds are sometimes used in decoctions as they contain a higher concentration of ursolic acid, a compound that may help to regulate menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried vitex agnus castus berries or 40-60 grams of fresh leaves and flowers.
Combine the gathered herbs with 1 liter of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 5-10 minutes or bring to a boil for 1-5 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica decoctions helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to regulate hormonal balance, particularly estrogen and progesterone levels. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as ligustilide and senkyunolide, have been shown to stimulate uterine contractions, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation. This can help to normalize menstrual cycles, alleviate symptoms of dysmenorrhea, and even induce menstruation in individuals with amenorrhea. Additionally, angelica's adaptogenic properties can help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to irregular periods.
remedy_properties: Regulatory effects on estrogen levels: Angelica decoctions have been shown to regulate estrogen levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea by promoting hormonal balance and ovulation.

Stimulating uterine contractions: The decoction's ability to stimulate uterine contractions can help regulate menstrual cycles and induce menstruation in cases of amenorrhea caused by uterine stagnation or hormonal imbalance.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica decoctions' antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, which can contribute to amenorrhea, and also protect against oxidative stress and damage that can disrupt menstrual cycles.
remedy_constituents: Falcarinol: A sesquiterpene, falcarinol has been shown to have estrogenic activity, which may help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea.
Falcarindiol: Similar to falcarinol, falcarindiol is also a sesquiterpene with estrogenic properties, which may help stimulate menstrual bleeding and alleviate amenorrhea.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): These compounds have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate menstrual irregularities associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are used for their high concentration of angelica's medicinal properties, which are believed to stimulate menstrual flow and help alleviate amenorrhea.
Leaves: They are used in decoctions for amenorrhea due to their high content of volatile oils, which may help to regulate hormonal balance and stimulate menstrual cycles.
Barks: The barks of the angelica plant are used to make decoctions for amenorrhea, as they are rich in saponins, which may help to stimulate uterine contractions and promote menstrual flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots and rhizomes of angelica archangelica in late summer or early fall.
Chop 1 to 2 teaspoons of fresh angelica archangelica root or 1 to 2 tablespoons of dried root into small pieces.
Combine chopped root with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes to allow the root's medicinal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with amenorrhea because it has a profound impact on the female reproductive system. The decoction is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins, which collectively help to stimulate uterine contractions, regulate hormonal imbalances, and increase blood flow to the pelvic region. This natural remedy can effectively address underlying causes of amenorrhea such as hormonal disorders, stress, and poor circulation, ultimately promoting regular menstrual cycles and alleviating symptoms associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to Amenorrhea by promoting hormonal balance and regulating menstrual cycles.

Vasodilatory effects: The decoction's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve blood flow to the uterus, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle and alleviating symptoms of Amenorrhea such as pain and fatigue.

Hormone-regulating properties: Ginkgo biloba has been traditionally used to regulate female hormone levels, including estrogen and progesterone, which can be imbalanced in cases of Amenorrhea, thereby helping to restore normal menstrual function.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Acts as a hormone regulator, helping to restore normal menstrual cycles by modulating the body's hormonal balance and improving endometrial receptivity.

Ginkgolides: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the hormonal imbalances and oxidative stress associated with amenorrhea, promoting a favorable environment for menstrual cycle regulation.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): Possesses estrogen-like properties, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea by promoting the growth and development of the endometrium.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which is believed to stimulate menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are often employed in traditional medicine for their potential to regulate menstrual cycles and stimulate ovulation, which can help treat amenorrhea.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are also used in decoctions for treating amenorrhea, possibly due to their mild estrogenic properties that can help regulate the menstrual cycle.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2-4 teaspoons of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and place them in a clean glass container.
Combine the measured ginkgo biloba with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has cooled to room temperature.
Strain the decoction using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Transfer the strained decoction to a glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese mayapple decoctions helps with amenorrhea because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate uterine contractions, promote blood flow to the pelvic area, and regulate hormonal imbalances. The decoction's primary active compound, podophyllotoxin, has been shown to increase estrogen levels and improve ovarian function, which can help induce menstruation in women with amenorrhea. Additionally, mayapple's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate uterine inflammation and scar tissue that may contribute to amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Emmenagogue property: Mayapple's emmenagogue property helps to stimulate menstruation, which can be beneficial in treating amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or uterine fibroids.

Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Mayapple's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties may help alleviate menstrual cramps, spasms, and pain associated with amenorrhea, promoting a smoother and more regular menstrual cycle.

Antioxidant and detoxifying effects: Mayapple's antioxidant and detoxifying properties may help to reduce the accumulation of toxins in the body, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances and menstrual irregularities that lead to amenorrhea.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with amenorrhea by regulating the menstrual cycle and improving blood flow to the uterus.
Isoflavones: They help with amenorrhea by stimulating the endometrium, promoting estrogen production, and regulating the menstrual cycle.
Flavonoids: They help with amenorrhea by reducing oxidative stress, promoting blood flow to the uterus, and regulating the menstrual cycle.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their warming, stimulating properties to promote blood flow and menstrual regularity.
Rhyzomes: Used for their cooling, soothing properties to calm the uterus and regulate menstrual cycles.
Leaves: Used for their sweet, astringent properties to balance the body's energy and promote menstrual flow.
remedy_recipe: Gather 15-30g of dried dysosma pleiantha roots and stems from a trusted source.
Cut the herbs into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes to prepare the water for infusion.
Steep the herb pieces in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release their properties.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to separate the solids from the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Raspberry decoctions helps with amenorrhea because they contain a unique combination of phytochemicals, particularly anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and vitamin C. These compounds have been shown to stimulate uterine contractions, improve blood flow to the uterus, and enhance hormonal balance. By regulating these physiological processes, herbal raspberry decoctions can help alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea, such as delayed or absent menstruation, cramps, and mood changes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Raspberry decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to menstrual irregularities and amenorrhea by promoting healthy uterine function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in raspberry decoctions, such as ellagic acid and anthocyanins, may help alleviate endometriosis-related inflammation, a common cause of amenorrhea, and promote menstrual regularity.

Uterotonic effects: Raspberry decoctions contain uterotonic compounds like fragarine, which can stimulate uterine contractions, helping to regulate menstruation and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea, such as heavy bleeding or prolonged periods.
remedy_constituents: Ellagic acid: This phenolic compound has been shown to have estrogenic activity, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin may help to reduce inflammation and promote uterine contractions, which can aid in regulating menstrual cycles.
Kaempferol: This flavonoid has been found to have estrogen-modulating effects, which may help to restore normal menstrual function in individuals experiencing amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rubus idaeus are used to make decoctions for amenorrhea because they contain emmenagogic properties that stimulate menstrual flow.
Leaves: The leaves of Rubus idaeus are used to make decoctions for amenorrhea because they are rich in estrogenic and progestogenic compounds that help regulate the menstrual cycle.
Barks: The barks of Rubus idaeus are used to make decoctions for amenorrhea because they contain stimulant and emmenagogic properties that help to induce menstrual flow.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 cups of fresh or dried rubus idaeus leaves and flowers for the decoction.
Rinse the rubus idaeus leaves and flowers with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the cleaned rubus idaeus leaves and flowers with 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper decoctions helps with amenorrhea because it stimulates uterine contractions, promoting regular menstrual cycles. The decoction's bioactive compounds, such as caryophyllene and eugenol, have a synergistic effect on the uterus, enhancing its function and improving hormonal balance. Additionally, monk's pepper has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce pelvic congestion and cramping associated with amenorrhea. By promoting uterine health and regulating menstrual cycles, monk's pepper decoctions can be an effective natural remedy for managing amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Monk's Pepper decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to amenorrhea by promoting a healthy uterine environment and regular menstrual cycles.

Hormone-regulating properties: Vitex agnus-castus has been shown to regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly those related to prolactin and estrogen levels, which can help restore normal menstrual function in women with amenorrhea.

Spasmolytic and anti-inflammatory properties: Monk's Pepper decoctions possess spasmolytic and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate cramps, spasms, and pain associated with menstruation, thereby supporting a more comfortable and regular menstrual cycle.
remedy_constituents: Lignans: Lignans help with amenorrhea by regulating estrogen levels and promoting uterine contractions, thereby inducing menstrual cycles.
Coumarins: Coumarins, specifically cnidilin and 4,7-dihydroxy-1-methoxy-isocoumarin, have been shown to have estrogenic activity, which can help stimulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea.

Flowers: May help regulate hormonal imbalances and stimulate menstrual flow due to their rich content of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried cnidium monnieri roots and place them in a heat-resistant container.
Boil 500ml of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5-7 minutes.
Add the dried cnidium monnieri roots to the boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Discard the solids and store the cnidium monnieri decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with amenorrhea because of its ability to balance the hormonal levels in the body, particularly estrogen and progesterone. The decoction's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to stimulate the production of luteinizing hormone, which regulates ovulation and menstruation. Additionally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the uterus and reduce cramping, making it a natural remedy for addressing amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or uterine issues.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root's main constituent, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory and estrogen-regulating effects, which can help to balance hormonal imbalances and stimulate menstruation.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root, such as isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe the uterine lining and regulate menstrual cycles.
Saponins: Licorice root's saponins can help to relax the smooth muscle of the uterus and improve blood flow, which can aid in increasing prostaglandins production and promoting menstruation.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and hormonal-balancing properties that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms.

Isoglycyrrhizic acid: A triterpenoid saponin similar to glycyrrhizin, isoglycyrrhizic acid has been found to stimulate the release of luteinizing hormone (LH), which plays a crucial role in regulating ovulation and menstrual cycles.

Licoricidin: A flavonoid glycoside, licoricidin has been found to have anti-stress and adaptogenic properties that help reduce cortisol levels, which can contribute to amenorrhea by disrupting hormonal balance and ovulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps to stimulate uterine contractions and improve menstrual flow.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Fruits: They are a good source of phytoestrogens, which can help to stimulate estrogen production and regulate menstrual cycles.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried roots of the plant in question and rinse with clean water.
Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5 to 10 minutes.
Add the dried roots to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

This can help stimulate menstrual flow in individuals with amenorrhea, particularly those who have experienced irregular or absent periods due to hormonal imbalances or uterine fibroids. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Paeonia x suffruticosa decoctions have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms.

Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, such as paeoniflorin, have been shown to have estrogen-like effects and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help regulate the menstrual cycle and alleviate amenorrhea.

remedy_recipe: Gather 30-60 grams of dried paeonia x suffruticosa roots and flowers for decoction.
Chop the paeonia x suffruticosa roots and flowers into small pieces for easier extraction.
Combine the chopped paeonia x suffruticosa with 2-3 liters of water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 10-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

The decoction's bitter compounds also help to regulate the digestive system, reducing inflammation and improving hormonal balance. remedy_recipe: Gather agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with cold running water for 5 minutes.
Measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried or 3 to 4 teaspoons of fresh material and place it in a pot.
Combine the measured agrimonia eupatoria with 1 quart of water in the pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5 to 10 minutes or until it reaches desired strength.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh leaves of taraxacum mongolicum at dawn when the plant is at its peak potency.
Wash the harvested leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of taraxacum mongolicum leaves in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen decoctions helps with amenorrhea because it has a regulating effect on the body's hormonal balance, particularly in women who experience irregular periods or delayed menstruation. remedy_recipe: Weigh out 30g of dried salvia miltiorrhiza root and stem, carefully cleaned and chopped into small pieces.
Combine the herb material with 600ml of distilled water in a large pot, bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing flavors and medicinal compounds to release.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days, refrigerate or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with amenorrhea because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that stimulate uterine contractions, promoting menstrual regularity. remedy_recipe: Measure 3-4 teaspoons of dried turmeric root or 6-8 grams of fresh turmeric root.
Combine the measured turmeric root with 250-500 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container at room temperature or refrigerate it.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried sesamum indicum seeds and add them to a glass cup.
Boil one cup of distilled water in a saucepan and pour it over the sesame seeds.
Simmer the mixture for 15 minutes on low heat, allowing the seeds to steep and release their active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another glass cup.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the uterus, reducing inflammation and cramps associated with menstrual irregularities.

Emollient property: Marshmallow's emollient properties help to soften and moisturize the uterine lining, promoting a healthy environment for implantation and reducing the risk of amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances.

Antioxidant property: Marshmallow's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to menstrual irregularities and amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant have been traditionally used to treat menstrual cramps and irregular periods, as they contain mucilage that helps to soothe and calm the uterine lining.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the marshmallow plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote hormonal balance, making them effective for treating amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis weighing approximately 10 grams.
Rinse the marshmallow root with cold running water and pat it dry with paper towels.
Combine the marshmallow root with one quart of boiling water in a non-reactive pot.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed decoctions helps with amenorrhea because of its rich estrogenic properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that may be contributing to the condition. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds present in chickweed also aid in reducing stress and promoting overall reproductive health. Additionally, chickweed's uterine toning effects can help stimulate blood flow and promote menstruation, making it a natural remedy for supporting menstrual regularity and alleviating symptoms of amenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chickweed's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce menstrual cramp discomfort and alleviate endometriosis-related inflammation, which may contribute to irregular or absent periods.

Estrogen-balancing effects: Chickweed contains phytoestrogens that may help regulate estrogen levels, potentially addressing hormonal imbalances that can cause amenorrhea by stimulating uterine contractions and promoting menstrual regularity.

Antispasmodic properties: Chickweed's antispasmodic compounds can relax the smooth muscles in the uterus, fallopian tubes, and ovaries, reducing cramps, spasms, and pain that may accompany amenorrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chickweed roots help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate cramps.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and phytoestrogens, chickweed seeds promote hormonal balance and uterus health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stellaria media flowers and leaves in a clean bucket for subsequent drying.
Dry the stellaria media herb material using a warm air circulation drying method at low temperature.
Grind dried stellaria media flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix one teaspoon of ground stellaria media with boiling water to create a decoction that steeps for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a clean cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese marsh marigold decoctions helps with amenorrhea because they have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles, promote uterine health, and stimulate blood flow. The herbs present in the decoction, such as Xue Fu Zhu Yu and Xi Xian Cao, are believed to tonify (nourish) the blood, remove stagnation, and restore balance to the menstrual system. This helps to alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea, including irregular periods, bloating, and mood changes, by promoting a healthy flow of energy in the body.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Marsh Marigold's diuretic property helps to increase urine output, which can help to stimulate menstrual flow and relieve symptoms of amenorrhea.

Emmenagogue property: Marsh Marigold has been traditionally used as an emmenagogue, meaning it promotes blood flow and stimulates the uterine muscles, which can help to regulate menstruation and alleviate Amenorrhea.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marsh Marigold's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding areas, which can contribute to amenorrhea symptoms such as painful periods or irregular bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Chinese Marsh Marigold have been used in traditional medicine to tonify the Liver and Kidney channels, promoting menstrual regularity and alleviating dysmenorrhea.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Chinese Marsh Marigold are believed to have a warming and nourishing effect on the uterus, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and ease menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Measure one hundred grams of dried ligularia przewalskii root slices and add them to a large pot.
Add eight cups of clean water to the pot, bringing it to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to medium low and simmer for thirty minutes to allow the medicinal compounds to extract.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another pot, discarding the solids.
Transfer the filtered decoction to individual containers, store it in the refrigerator for up to three days, and administer two times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild asparagus decoctions helps with amenorrhea because they possess unique phytochemicals that stimulate the reproductive system. The decoction's diuretic properties help to remove toxins and excess fluids from the body, which can contribute to menstrual irregularities. Additionally, its hormonal balancing effects regulate estrogen levels, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle. As a result, wild asparagus decoctions have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat amenorrhea, restoring regular menstrual function and alleviating related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic and antistrogenic effects: The decoction of Wild Asparagus has been shown to have estrogenic properties, which could potentially help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate amenorrhea symptoms. Its antistrogenic effects may also help balance excessive estrogen levels.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions: Wild Asparagus contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hormonal imbalances and irregular menstruation.

Gonadotropic and uterotropic properties: The decoction has been traditionally used to stimulate ovarian function and improve uterine tone, which could potentially help restore normal menstrual cycles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in phytoestrogens, which help regulate hormonal imbalances and stimulate menstrual flow.
Rhizomes: Contain saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Collect and clean the asparagus cochinchinensis roots and rhizomes weighing about 30 grams each time.
Chop the prepared asparagus cochinchinensis into small pieces and soak them in distilled water for at least eight hours.
Boil the soaked asparagus cochinchinensis in six cups of distilled water for approximately 45 minutes to an hour or until the liquid reduces by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids after filtering.
Allow the herbal decoction to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it in a sealed glass bottle for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild strawberry decoctions helps with amenorrhea because they stimulate blood flow and hormonal balance in the body. The herbs, such as Fragaria vesca, contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders. By increasing circulation and regulating hormone levels, wild strawberry decoctions may help to induce or regularize menstruation in individuals with amenorrhea. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the decoction may help to reduce inflammation and promote overall reproductive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and Antioxidant Properties: Wild strawberry decoction contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to regulate menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms associated with amenorrhea.

Astringent and Hemostatic Properties: The astringent properties of wild strawberry can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, while its hemostatic properties can promote clotting and stop excessive bleeding, making it effective in treating heavy menstrual bleeding (HMB) associated with amenorrhea.

Uterine Tonic and Stimulant Properties: Wild strawberry decoction has been traditionally used as a uterine tonic and stimulant to regulate menstruation, increase blood flow to the uterus, and stimulate ovulation, making it helpful in treating amenorrhea caused by hormonal imbalances or uterine issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea.
Rhyzomes: Contain essential oils and flavonoids that stimulate uterine contractions and promote hormonal balance, making them effective for treating amenorrhea.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried fragaria vesca leaves and stems measuring one ounce each.
Combine the fragaria vesca ingredients with four cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 20 minutes allowing flavors to extract fully.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a large glass measuring cup.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to amenorrhea that are treated with herbal decoctions are dysmenorrhea, menorrhagia, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Decoctions made from herbs such as turmeric, ginger, and cinnamon can help alleviate symptoms of dysmenorrhea, while those containing raspberry leaf and shepherd's purse may help regulate heavy menstrual bleeding in menorrhagia. Additionally, herbal decoctions made with chasteberry and dandelion root have been traditionally used to address hormonal imbalances associated with PCOS.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with amenorrhea is a blend of Red Clover, Black Cohosh, and Vitex. Red Clover contains isoflavones that mimic estrogen, supporting hormonal balance. Black Cohosh helps regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms associated with hormonal imbalances. Vitex, also known as Chasteberry, stimulates the pituitary gland to release hormones that regulate menstruation. Drinking a decoction of these herbs, ideally in a ratio of 2:1:1, may help restore menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of amenorrhea. Consult a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by BioMed research international, herbal decoctions can be beneficial for amenorrhea due to the presence of emmenagogue plants in traditional Persian medicine (TPM). The study, conducted in 2017, searched Iranian traditional medicine literature and electronic databases to gather information on herbal medications for oligomenorrhea and amenorrhea, ultimately identifying 87 emmenagogue medicinal plants in TPM that were found to be effective in treating these conditions. By incorporating these herbal decoctions into treatment plans, women may experience a return of menstrual cycles, alleviating the symptoms of amenorrhea.
